payload.text:
너는 OBD 로그 분석기잖아?

내가 Android 개발자인데 앱 내에서 응답값을 테스트 해보려고 해.

만약 txt 파일에 연결 성공해서 차량과 통신한 로그가 있는데,

시작 시간과 종료 시간 선택하면 그 시간 내 응답한 로그가 있으면, 요쳥 명령 별로 응답 값을 리스트로 해줘.

예를 들어보자

//7DF, 01 0D : 7E903410D00/7E803410D00//>, delay : 52ms, MOBDDataPush / isActivityVisible : true (64MB/512MB)
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:027]//dataWrite : 01 23
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:083]//7DF, 01 23 : 7E80441230155//>, delay : 55ms, MOBDDataPush / isActivityVisible : true (64MB/512MB)
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:087]//dataWrite : 01 07
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:144]//7DF, 01 07 : 7E80341078F//>, delay : 56ms, MOBDDataPush / isActivityVisible : true (64MB/512MB)
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:149]//dataWrite : 01 0D
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:205]//7DF, 01 0D : 7E903410D00/7E803410D00//>, delay : 56ms, MOBDDataPush / isActivityVisible : true (65MB/512MB)
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:211]//dataWrite : 01 49
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:264]//7DF, 01 49 : 7E903414900/7E803414925//>, delay : 54ms, MOBDDataPush / isActivityVisible : true (65MB/512MB)
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:269]//dataWrite : 01 0D
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:324]//7DF, 01 0D : 7E903410D00/7E803410D00//>, delay : 54ms, MOBDDataPush / isActivityVisible : true (65MB/512MB)
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:328]//dataWrite : 01 0B
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:386]//7DF, 01 0B : 7E803410B1E//>, delay : 58ms, MOBDDataPush / isActivityVisible : true (66MB/512MB)
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:392]//dataWrite : 01 0C
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:458]//7DF, 01 0C : 7E904410C086C/7E804410C086C//>, delay : 66ms, MOBDDataPush / isActivityVisible : true (66MB/512MB)
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:464]//dataWrite : 01 0D
[2025-12-09 10:44:15:519

이렇게 있다고 한다면 

총 요청 명령은 "01 0D", "01 23", "01 07", "01 49", ""01 0B", ""01 49" 이렇게 있을 테고 그 사이에 01 0D 는 3번 요청했으니 해당되는 리스트에 {"7E903410D00/7E803410D00//>","7E903410D00/7E803410D00//>","7E903410D00/7E803410D00//>"} 이렇게 들어가있겠네

이거를 상세 세션 정보 옆에 탭 추가로 만들고,

먼저 시작/종료 시간 선택 하고 확인 하면 보여주도록 만들어줘

**Analyzing User Request**

I'm currently focused on the user's objective: incorporating OBD2 command simulation within a defined timeframe into the Infocar Log Analyzer.  My initial assessment indicates an Android developer persona. I am carefully evaluating the specific functionalities required to simulate and analyze the responses, emphasizing the temporal element of the request.


**Defining Simulation Parameters**

I've been working on defining the input and output requirements for the OBD2 command simulation feature. My focus is on the core logic: filtering logs based on time, identifying write commands and responses, grouping responses by command, and presenting the raw response data. I'm also planning the UI layout - a dedicated tab with date/time pickers and a button to trigger the simulation.


**Implementing Simulator Tab**

My focus has shifted to the UI and logic for the OBD2 simulator. I'm creating `ResponseSimulator.tsx`, which will reside within a new tab managed by `Dashboard.tsx`.  I'm devising the Regex and logic to pair commands and responses efficiently. This involves parsing write commands and associating them with their corresponding responses from the log data. The UI will include date/time selection to initiate simulations.


**Developing Regex for Parsing**

I'm currently working on the regex and parsing logic within `logParser.ts`. I've extracted the message part and am refining the regex to accurately capture both commands (like `01 0D`) and their corresponding responses (e.g., `7E903410D00/7E803410D00`). The initial regex might be too specific, so I'm revisiting the log examples. My strategy is to first identify `dataWrite : [CMD]` lines and then look for subsequent response lines containing the same command in the format `[CMD] : [RESPONSE]//>`.


**Designing UI Integration**

I'm currently focused on integrating the simulator tab into the existing dashboard. Adding the "SIMULATOR" tab to `Dashboard.tsx` is straightforward, and rendering the `ResponseSimulator` component when active is the next step. I'm keeping the data structure local to the component initially, to reduce file modifications, and will only consider updating `types.ts` if sharing the structure becomes necessary.


**Adapting Parsing Logic**

I'm refining my parsing strategy within `logParser.ts`. I'm now focusing on directly parsing the response lines, as they consistently contain both the command and the response data, simplifying the pairing process. The initial regex was a good starting point, but I've updated it to better capture the command and response from the log entries, specifically using the pattern `(?:7DF,\s)?([0-9A-F\s]+)\s:\s(.*?)(?=\/\/|$)`. I'm confident this new approach will improve accuracy and efficiency.


**Implementing Date Formatting**

I'm currently focused on the `datetime-local` input type and its interaction with Date objects. I've realized I need a helper function to convert Date objects from the metadata into the required `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` string format for the default values. I'm working on a `toLocalISOString(date)` function to handle this conversion and avoid timezone complications. I'm also ensuring the styling adheres to the existing Tailwind and Lucide icon guidelines. I'm now thinking about how to display the simulator's output.


**Adding Simulator Tab**

I've successfully updated `Dashboard.tsx` to include the simulator tab and import the `ResponseSimulator` component.  My focus is now shifting to ensure the UI handles the output from the regex parsing correctly and displays the responses for a given command. I'm carefully considering how to structure the data for optimal user readability and interaction with the simulated data.  I've realized I need to refine how I present the information.


**Crafting Output Logic**

I'm now focused on the exact presentation of the simulation results. My priority is to accurately reflect the user's focus on the log message, including both the command and response data. I've successfully extracted the required information using the regex `/^.*,\s*([0-9A-F\s]{2,})\s*:\s*(.*?\/\/>),/`.  I'm now integrating this output string within the simulator's results to mirror the log format.

payload.text:
시작과 종료를 각각 선택하지말고,

하나의 시크바로 만들어줘.

그리고 시크바에 연결 시작과 종료가 표기 되어야 해.

예를들어 로그파일의 시작이 오전 1시 16분이고 종료가 오후 4시 23분이야.

근데 오전 3시 20분 시작~오전 3시 33분 종료 오전 4시 11분 시작 오전 7시 종료면

각각 그 지점이 떠야한다 이말이야.

**Simplifying the Input**

I'm currently focused on simplifying the user input for the ResponseSimulator component. I've analyzed the request and determined the primary goal is to replace two separate input fields with a single range slider. The key feature is the ability to select a continuous range of time instead of specifying two discrete points. This change should streamline the user experience significantly.


**Visualizing Connection States**

I'm now diving into the visualization of "Connection Sessions" on the slider. Considering use case examples, the slider track needs to visually represent the periods where the app was actively connected to the vehicle. This includes accurately marking these connected regions and designing a track that handles these visual representations correctly. The goal is to provide a clear and intuitive representation of connected periods.


**Defining Connection Periods**

I've shifted focus to defining the connection periods precisely. My latest approach involves prioritizing the explicit `LifecycleEvent`s with type 'CONNECTION'. I'll filter these events for success and disconnect/failure messages to construct an array of connection start and end dates. This data will directly inform the slider's visual representation of active connection periods.


**Defining Slider Structure**

I've been working on defining the structure of the custom range slider. After some research, I've decided a React component approach with a relative container and absolute-positioned elements offers the best balance of flexibility and control. This allows for precise handle placement and visual representation of connected periods. The plan now involves breaking down the slider into distinct divs: the background, connected segments, the selected range, and the draggable handles. The input will be the logs and lifecycle events, with the state representing the selection range percentages.


**Developing the UI Elements**

I'm now implementing the UI elements. The track will be gray, the connection blocks green, and the selection a semi-transparent blue. I've built a `calculateConnectionPeriods` helper, looping through logs to find "connected state : 2" (start) and "disconnect" (end). Edge cases are handled for incomplete periods. The slider component uses container refs for width calculations and mouse event handlers for draggable handles.


**Improving Connection Logic**

I'm now refining the connection detection logic. Simply using "Connected" events isn't sufficient. My latest idea is to leverage `LogCategory.OBD` or specific `7DF` messages to determine connection activity. The new algorithm buckets logs into minutes and marks active minutes with OBD data. Adjacent active minutes will then be merged into connection ranges, providing a more accurate representation of data traffic.


**Outlining the Final Structure**

I've outlined the final structure for the `ResponseSimulator.tsx` file.  This includes an `extractActiveRanges` function to calculate percentages for active ranges from the logs. I'll have the `RangeSlider` component itself, with props for min, max, value, and ranges. The render will include track, active ranges, selected range, and handles. The main render will bring it all together with header, slider, time display, action button, and results.

**Refining Logic and Edge Cases**

I'm now focusing on error handling. I've identified the issue of handles crossing during dragging and implemented a fix to prevent the left handle from exceeding the right and vice versa.  Also, to address the potential divide by zero error for single-log files, I'll add a guard.

**Building the XML**

I'm now translating the structure into code, focusing on updating the `components/ResponseSimulator.tsx` file. For styling, I will use Tailwind to efficiently manage the absolute positioning and visual aspects. Additionally, I need to incorporate the previously requested icon update.


**Finalizing Timeline Visualization**

I'm now integrating the session visualization directly into the timeline slider. Rather than strictly connection state, I'll display "Active Communication" periods, determined by OBD data traffic, as requested. The plan is to parse logs for min/max times, identify activity clusters (sessions), and render these as colored bars on the slider track. The parent component's state will then update the start/end times based on the slider's position, ensuring the displayed timeline reflects the active communication periods.


**Parsing for Data Integrity**

I'm currently revising the `calculateConnectionPeriods` logic to ensure data integrity. I'm introducing checks to eliminate spurious or incomplete log entries. Specifically, I'm focusing on ensuring valid pairs of connection start and end events. This will prevent incorrect period calculations. To achieve this, I'm adjusting how connection periods are constructed based on the log entries.

사용자님, 좋은 아이디어입니다! 시작/종료 시간을 텍스트로 입력하는 것보다, 전체 로그 타임라인에서 실제 **통신이 발생한 구간(연결 구간)**을 시각적으로 보고 범위를 드래그하여 선택하는 것이 훨씬 직관적입니다.

`ResponseSimulator.tsx`를 전면 수정하여 다음 기능을 구현하겠습니다:

5. 그리고 시크바의 UI 를 좀더 크게 만들어줘

**Assessing the OBD Data**

I've been diving into the user's request, focusing on improving the `ResponseSimulator`. The core problem seems to be analyzing and working with OBD logs. I'm trying to get a better grasp on exactly how the user expects the `ResponseSimulator` to handle this data.


**Implementing the Simulation Logic**

I'm now fully immersed in the core logic for the response simulation. I've broken down the user's requirements and started implementing them, starting with the edge cases. I've got the "No Results" message showing when no data matches the criteria. The progress indicator is up next, to handle those times the parsing might take a moment.


**Fine-tuning the Green Bars**

I'm presently focusing on refining the logic for the green bars in the interface.  I've shifted my attention to the parsing of `0100` messages, aiming to accurately represent valid communication sessions.  I've been thinking about how the presence or absence of error keywords in the responses should influence the visualization of the data.  I need to ensure the logic reflects the user's intent. The seekbar will be the next thing, after the current improvements.


**Prioritizing Valid Sessions**

I'm now carefully examining the green bar logic. The user wants to start the session indicator at the first valid `0100` response. However, I'm considering that the green bars could also represent *any* valid OBD data flow. It's becoming clearer that I need to refine how I define "valid" data.  I'll ensure the presence of `0100` is a strong trigger for the session start, along with other valid OBD responses. This way, the timeline will accurately show the communication flow.


**Refining the Error Filtering**

I'm now focusing on improving the error filtering within the logs.  I'll include *any* valid OBD response as "communication", and exclude specific error keywords the user wants to filter. I've also wrapped the `handleAnalyze` logic to allow a loading spinner to show. I'm making sure to implement the UI adjustments too, to increase the size of the slider's container, track, and handle. Finally, I'll add the formatted timestamp text as requested.


**Enhancing Data Communication**

I'm working on the data analysis, starting with an improved `handleAnalyze` function to correctly bucket the data, updating the UI accordingly. I'm focusing on the slider design and "No Results" display, and I'll add a loading spinner to give users visual feedback while parsing the logs. I'm also refining my approach to the `01 00` logic, ensuring green bars represent a *valid* communication, not just the initial connection.


**Defining Valid Responses**

I'm now implementing the criteria for "valid" OBD responses.  I've decided to consider a response valid if it is an OBD message that doesn't contain the pre-defined error keywords. This should solve the "not NO DATA..." issue and correctly handle typical `0100` responses that lack these errors. I've also begun to implement the loading spinner using Tailwind and the timestamp display using a grid layout.


**Optimizing Data Visualization**

I'm currently focused on defining the `isValidLog` criteria to correctly filter valid OBD data. I'm prioritizing successful `0100` responses to determine the start of a session, but also accounting for general valid data flows.  I've added the side panel for the time text and adjusted UI sizes.  I am ensuring that "data" includes any OBD message that does not include pre-defined errors.  The active ranges now reflect only buckets where the `isValidLog` function returns true.


**Refining the Response Definition**

I've revised the criteria for valid responses. Now, any OBD message that excludes specific error keywords will be flagged as valid, including the typical `0100` responses the user mentioned. This approach ensures broader coverage and avoids the "NO DATA" issue, accurately representing valid data flow within the application. I'm focusing on the `isValidResponse` to use this definition.

사용자님의 요청 사항을 반영하여 `ResponseSimulator.tsx`를 업데이트하겠습니다.

**변경 사항 요약:**

payload.text:
1. 연결 유효 구간의 시작 지점은 0100 의 정상응답 이후 01 0D 명령이 시작 되는 구간 ~ 종료 지점은 이후 connected_Finish 가 나올 때 까지 로 해줘.

2. 시크바의 슬라이더가 너무 커. 마커처럼 시크바 안이 아니라 그 바로 위에서 가르키게 해줘.

3. 응답 값이 길면 너무 스크롤하기 귀찮으니까 접었다 폈다 할 수 있게 해주고 디폴트는 접힌 채로 전달해줘.

4. 타임라인 탭 아래로 스크롤 할 수 있게 화면 bottom 에 패딩 좀 더 추가해줘

**Improving Range Detection**

My focus has shifted to the refinement of the valid connection range detection. I'm currently working through the implications of a more specific implementation, particularly the need to clearly define the starting point after a successful `0100` response. This is a crucial step towards making the logic more robust. I'm considering all the scenarios to ensure no edge cases are missed.


**Addressing Session Boundaries**

I've been concentrating on pinpointing the exact start and end of a valid OBD session, specifically after receiving the `0100` response. My focus is on ensuring the green bars accurately reflect this session logic, and not just any OBD/BLE activity. It's a key requirement that the visualization clearly represents the beginning of the `010D` (Speed PID) data streams, all the way to a `connected_Finish` event. This has implications for the overall UI presentation of active sessions.


**Developing Session Visualization**

I'm now deep-diving into the visual representation of the sessions. The goal is to clearly highlight when a valid OBD session begins after `0100` and `010D` events, extending to either `connected_Finish` or disconnection. The logic to handle session boundaries is taking shape, employing a state machine with states like `WAITING_FOR_0100`, `WAITING_FOR_010D`, `IN_SESSION`, and `ENDED`.  I'm focusing on UI updates for the slider, making it more intuitive and visually appealing with a pin-style marker and the new `CollapsedResult` component. Adding padding for scrolling room.


**Identifying Active Drive**

I've homed in on using the "Speed (01 0D)" polling loop as a key indicator for "Active Drive." For the slider, I'm adjusting the handle style from its current absolute positioning to a pin or triangle shape sitting on top of the track. Regarding collapsible results, I'll wrap the `arrayString` div conditionally, and introduce a "Show full data" button. I've also begun chronological log scanning in `ResponseSimulator.tsx` to refine the `isInsideSession` flag, starting a session when a non-error `01 0D` is detected.


**Refining Session Ranges**

My latest focus has been on improving the session range logic. I'm building a range list by parsing logs, looking for key events like '01 00' followed by '01 0D'.  Valid '01 0D' messages with a '01 00' precursor now confirm the start of a session, leading to more accurate range identification. I'm also addressing the visual representation: updating the slider's handle and track to enhance user understanding. Collapsible results are under review for a better presentation. I'm also adding padding to the main container.


**Implementing Robust Session Parsing**

I'm now refining the log parsing to handle variations in how `01 00` is represented, looking for patterns like `41 00` (response) or general `01 00` requests. I've switched to utilizing the parser's `message` to catch these nuances.  The algorithm is now: Locate valid `01 00` responses, then find the first `01 0D` immediately after to determine `T_start`. `T_end` is either a `connected_Finish` or the end of the logs. I'm also planning for scenarios with multiple sessions in a single log file. The slider's handle is being adjusted to visually point downward for clarity. The container will also have a margin-top. payload.text:
아 이게 map 이 너무 거대해서 JVM 의 최대 사이즈를 넘어가서 빌드가 안 돼.

어떤식으로 해야 될까?

JSON? 아니면 다른 방식?

**Evaluating Code Complexity**

I've been analyzing the issue with the `ObdMockData.kt` file. The primary concern is that a `mapOf` function is generating too much code, leading to compilation failures. I'm focusing on why this is occurring and the potential solutions, like breaking the map into smaller chunks or adopting a more efficient data structure.


**Investigating JSON Alternatives**

I'm now evaluating JSON as a solution to the compilation issues. I think it's the strongest contender because of its flexibility with large datasets and straightforward resource management within the project. I need to figure out how to parse the JSON data and implement a strategy for placing it in the app's structure (assets or raw). I am also considering alternatives to JSON.


**Exploring Chunked Methods**

My current plan, to deal with the compilation limits, is to split the `mapOf` initialization into modular chunks, like `init1()` and `init2()`, then merge them. However, I'm finding this approach messy and just a temporary bandage, since this approach may increase the APK size and compilation time. Also, I'm considering alternatives to JSON.


**Devising Data Initialization**

I'm now zeroing in on a user-friendly solution, shifting focus from pure code generation.  My initial JSON plan faced file management issues. I'm now exploring a Kotlin class that chunks data, avoiding massive maps and potential compilation problems. This offers a "copy-paste" experience while mitigating code size constraints.  I'm also considering generating JSON alongside Kotlin code for flexibility.


**Implementing Method Splitting**

I'm now prioritizing method splitting, as it preserves the simple "copy-paste" user experience. The mechanism involves breaking the large map initialization into smaller init methods within the `ObdMockData` object. This addresses the bytecode limit for single methods, although I'm aware that extremely large datasets could still lead to class file size or compilation speed issues. For optimal data handling, I'm leaning toward combining JSON data generation with corresponding Kotlin loader code.


**Finalizing JSON Integration**

My current focus is on finalizing JSON integration as the primary solution. I'm aiming for a streamlined user experience and ensuring stability by addressing the JVM size limits. The plan involves generating a JSON asset and a corresponding Kotlin loader class. I'll modify the `ResponseSimulator.tsx` to generate the JSON data and provide a helper class template that loads from the asset. The UI will guide the user through saving the JSON and copying the Kotlin loader, ensuring a smooth process and user-friendly experience.


**Optimizing Data Handling**

I'm now prioritizing the JSON asset file approach because it's the professional Android method and offers better performance for larger datasets, and helps me avoid method size limits. I'm exploring encoding the data as a long string inside a class, parsed at runtime, for a single file copy-paste experience, but I'm concerned it may be risky. My decision is to go with a JSON asset file approach. I plan to split the result view into two parts or toggle for the user to view the JSON data and a corresponding Kotlin loader class. I'll focus on the JSON generation and loading logic next.


**Considering Asset Integration**

I've been re-evaluating the `ObdMockData` setup. The previous design, as an object, presented issues with `Context` dependencies. Storing data in separate classes looked messy, so I'm now leaning towards an asset-based strategy. The workflow involves the user generating JSON, placing it in the `assets` folder, and using a copied Kotlin class that requires `Context` for initialization. I'm also exploring a "code-only" split approach for users who prefer fewer files, which would involve breaking the `mapOf` into chunked init methods.


**Refining Asset Implementation**

I'm now prioritizing the JSON asset-based solution. The previous split approach, although simpler for users to copy/paste code, still had issues regarding large map initialization. To handle the `Context` dependency and ensure streamlined data loading, I'm focusing on the JSON asset approach. My workflow involves the user generating JSON data in `ResponseSimulator.tsx`, which can then be placed into the `assets` folder, and an automatically generated Kotlin loader class to handle parsing and access. I'm focusing on the design and implementation of JSON asset generation and a dynamic Kotlin loader class.


**Integrating JSON Loading**

I'm now integrating JSON loading as the primary approach. Using `org.json.JSONObject` will keep dependencies light and handle potential performance issues and complexities that come with other libraries. I am also planning the UI with a tab to display JSON data and a corresponding Kotlin class to streamline data handling and user experience. Also I am implementing the Android-specific code.



**Integrating Asset Generation**

I'm now focusing on integrating the JSON file generation and Kotlin helper class. My current plan involves updating the `ResponseSimulator.tsx` to generate `obd_mock_data.json` and a lightweight `ObdMockData.kt` file. I'm also revising the UI to offer tabs, one for viewing the JSON data and another for the Kotlin code, and including a copy button. I'll focus on crafting robust Kotlin code and incorporating the JSON.stringify. I am planning the integration within `ResponseSimulator.tsx`, ensuring proper JSON formatting and user-friendliness.


**Developing UI Tabs**

I'm now focusing on the UI with tabs to render the JSON data and a corresponding Kotlin loader class for ease of use. I'm leveraging `org.json` for a lightweight and simple implementation. My recent updates include adding a state `viewMode` that toggles between 'JSON' and 'KOTLIN', implementing a `generateJsonString` function, and refactoring `generateKotlinCode` to represent the loader logic using the JSON library. I'm focusing on the integration of the code and the UI.


**Completing Code Generation**

I'm finishing the development of the UI tabs, focusing on ensuring the generated JSON and Kotlin code are presented cleanly and are copy-paste friendly. I have a `viewMode` state that toggles between 'JSON' and 'KOTLIN' states. I implemented a new `generateKotlinCode` function for a better Kotlin loader class representation. I'm focusing on the final integration of the JSON library and the UI rendering logic.

맵의 크기가 너무 커서 JVM의 **"Code too large"** (메소드 크기 64KB 제한) 또는 힙 메모리 오류가 발생하는 것입니다.

이 문제를 해결하는 가장 좋은 방법은 **데이터(JSON)와 로직(Kotlin)을 분리**하는 것입니다.

1.  **데이터**: 대용량 응답 데이터를 `obd_mock_data.json` 파일로 저장 (Android `assets` 폴더).
2.  **로직**: `ObdMockData` 클래스는 이 파일을 읽어서 메모리에 로드하는 역할만 수행.

`ResponseSimulator.tsx`를 수정하여 **두 가지 결과물(JSON 데이터, Kotlin 로더)**을 탭으로 구분하여 제공하도록 변경하겠습니다.

이제 개발자님은:
1.  **JSON 탭**의 내용을 복사하여 `src/main/assets/obd_mock_data.json` 파일을 만드시고,

오케이 앞으로는 데이터 추출하기 누르면 데이터 JSON 만 전달해줘. 
로더 클래스는 추가해놨어.

**Refining the Output**

I'm currently focused on the specific output requirements.  The goal is to streamline the response simulator.  Instead of offering multiple options, the "Extract" action now needs to exclusively provide the JSON data.  This modification is designed to improve the directness of the output.


**Removing Unnecessary Elements**

I've made progress on removing the Kotlin loader generation. The plan is to remove the "Kotlin Loader" tab and related generation logic from the UI to streamline the workflow. In `components/ResponseSimulator.tsx`, I'll remove the `generatedKotlin` and `activeTab` states, the `generateKotlinLoaderCode` function, and the tab navigation. I'll also simplify the "Copy" logic and update the file path display. Finally, the "Analyze" logic will no longer generate Kotlin code.


**Eliminating Unneeded Steps**

I'm focused on streamlining the `handleAnalyze` function. My objective is to have it exclusively call `generateJson` to directly produce the necessary JSON data. I'm removing the parts dealing with Kotlin generation. I'm also planning to simplify the rendering logic to reflect these changes, specifically updating the file path display.


**Consolidating UI Changes**

I'm focused on the UI changes. I've simplified the display of the JSON output and updated the copy button. The Kotlin generation code is now removed, focusing the display solely on the JSON. I've also verified that the core functionalities, such as the range slider and analysis, remain unaffected by these alterations. The aim is to have a clear and efficient interface for extracting JSON data. Finally, I've checked the XML output formatting to ensure it is correctly structured.

네, 로더 클래스를 이미 적용하셨다면 반복적으로 생성할 필요가 없으니, **JSON 데이터만 깔끔하게 추출**하도록 `ResponseSimulator` 컴포넌트를 경량화하겠습니다.

탭(Tab) UI를 제거하고, 분석 즉시 JSON 데이터를 보여주며 바로 복사할 수 있도록 수정합니다.

payload.text:
분석 할 때 연결 문제면 

안녕하십니까 인포카입니다.
이용에 불편함을 드려 죄송의 말씀 드립니다.
​
고객님께서 프로토콜로 인한 연결 관련 어려움을 겪으신 것으로 보입니다.
네트워크가 원활한 상태에서 설정 -> 스캐너 연결 -> 상단의 "연결 문제 확인하기" 탭을 누르신 뒤 해당 내용 확인 부탁 드립니다.

또한 
1. 앱 내 [설정] -> [프로토콜&데이터] 기능을 통해 [응답 대기시간] 및 [재연결 횟수] 늘리기.
2. [프로토콜 종류]에서 프로토콜을 순차적으로 바꾸면서 데이터가 정상적으로 표시되어지는지 확인 부탁 드립니다. (먼저 6번 시도 부탁 드립니다.)

추가적으로 다른 궁금하신 사항이 있으시다면 앱 메인 페이지에서 "사용 가이드" 확인 부탁 드립니다.
감사합니다.

이 답변을,

01 0D, 01 0C 등 표준 요청 명령에 계속해서 NO DATA 나오면

안녕하십니까 인포카입니다.
이용에 불편함을 드려 죄송의 말씀 드립니다.

고객님께서 호환되는 프로토콜과 연결이 되지 않아 "NO DATA" 가 응답 되며, 반복적인 비정상 응답으로인한 연결 종료가 된 것으로 보입니다.

아래 사항 참고 부탁 드립니다.
1. 앱 내 [설정] -> [프로토콜&데이터] 기능을 통해 [응답 대기시간] 및 [재연결 횟수] 늘리기.
2. [프로토콜 종류]에서 프로토콜을 순차적으로 바꾸면서 데이터가 정상적으로 표시되어지는지 확인 부탁 드립니다. (먼저 6번 시도 부탁 드립니다.)

그럼에도 데이터가 정상적으로 나오지 않는다면 고객님의 차량과 호환이 되지 않아 데이터가 나오지 않는것으로 판단됩니다.

추가적으로 다른 궁금하신 사항이 있으시다면 앱 메인 페이지에서 "사용 가이드" 확인 부탁 드립니다.

감사합니다.

이 답변을,

WI FI OBD 스캐너 썼는데 연결 문제가 나오면

안녕하세요 인포카입니다.

Wi-Fi 모듈 스캐너 연결에 어려움을 겪으신 것으로 보입니다.
이용에 불편함을 드려 죄송의 말씀 드립니다.

해당 연결의 경우 
1. 디바이스 시스템 설정에서 Wi-Fi 와 Wi-Fi 스캐너와 연결
2. 그 후 인포카 앱 내에서 올바른 IP와 포트 연결 시도 부탁드립니다.

자세한 사항은 앱 내 사용 가이드 - 연결 부분 참고 부탁 드립니다.

감사합니다

이 답변을,

ATZ 에서 정상 응답이 아닌 많은 응답이 온다면

안녕하세요 인포카입니다
먼저 이용에 불편함을 드려서 죄송합니다
보내주신 로그파일을 보았을때에  HUD 제품과 같이사용하시는 것으로 보입니다
HUD 제품과 동시에 사용 시 제품 간 데이터 충돌로 인해 데이터 수집이 어려울 수 있습니다.
Y 케이블을 사용하게 되면, 두 가지 제품에서 동시에 데이터 호출하여 ECU에서 정상적인 데이터를 보내지 않을 수 있습니다.
obd단자함에 인포카스캐너만 연결해주시기 바랍니다
감사합니다.


이 답변을 연결 리포트를 2분할 해서 우측에다가 적어줘.
복사할 수 있게도 해줘.

단 분석할 때 컨트리 코드 획득하잖아. 그걸 기준으로 번역해서 적어줘

**Analyzing the Requirement**

I'm currently dissecting the request. The primary goal is integrating a "Customer Service Response Generator" into the Dashboard. Crucially, the system should trigger response generation based on diagnosis data. I'm focusing on the logical flow from diagnosis to response.


**Formulating the Responses**

I've crafted a set of responses for common scenarios. They cover connection issues, Protocol Incompatibility, Wi-Fi connectivity problems, and ATZ response noise. Now focusing on UI integration, I envision a split-column layout for the "Connection Report" with technical diagnosis on the left and the customer service response on the right. A "Copy" button is a critical addition. Localization, guided by a detected country code, is the final element I'm working through.


**Developing the Logic**

I'm now focusing on integrating the proposed CS response generator.  I've updated the `ConnectionDiagnosis` interface in `types.ts` to accommodate the CS response data and settled on passing "Issue Type" to the view for translation.  I've started updating `logParser.ts` to extract the country code and detect the relevant scenarios from OBD2 logs.  Finally, I'll rework `ConnectionStatus.tsx` into a two-column layout.


**Building the Response Generator**

I'm deep into implementing the response generation logic. The key is now crafting a `translationMap` to handle Korean and fallback to English. The "Copy" button is integrated. I am designing specific scenarios for the log parser.  Scenario 1 (Wi-Fi), Scenario 2 (HUD/Y-Cable), Scenario 3 (NO DATA), and the default "General Connection" case all need to be properly implemented.


**Defining Country Code Logic**

I'm now integrating the country code determination. I'll modify the `parseLogFile` function to explicitly look for "Country" keys in the logs, defaulting to English if the country is not specified.  I am also setting up the translation dictionary with both English and Korean. I'm deep diving on defining the CS response logic.


**Integrating the Types**

I've incorporated `csDiagnosisType` into `ConnectionDiagnosis` within `types.ts`, defining specific scenarios like `HUD_INTERFERENCE` and `NO_DATA_PROTOCOL`. Now I'm focusing on updating `logParser.ts` to implement detailed scenario checks. I'll filter `ATZ` responses for HUD interference, count 'NO DATA' occurrences for data issues, and look for "WiFi" indicators in the logs. Finally, I'll extract the country code within `parseLogFile`.


**Developing the Scenarios**

I'm translating the existing responses into Korean and have mapped those specific cases.  Now, I'm working to add scenarios, which are currently only in English, such as: "Hello, this is Infocar... It seems like a protocol issue... check 'Connection Diagnosis' tab... Try increasing timeout... Try Protocol 6..." for *General* cases. I'm adding `No Data`, `WiFi`, and `HUD` cases. I'll define these within the context of the current structure.


**Refining Log Parsing**

I'm now revising the log parser logic to refine scenario detection. For *HUD interference*, I'll check for an `ATZ` response followed by other PIDs or an excessively long response. For *Wi-Fi*, I'll check for the connection type. I'll utilize `isError` or re-scan for *No Data* scenarios, and look for `userCountry` for the country code.


**Generating the XML**

I'm now focused on constructing the XML structure for the customer service responses.  I'll ensure the XML schema aligns with the defined scenarios: "General Connection," "No Data," "WiFi," and "HUD."  I'm also considering potential future expansion, ensuring the XML design is flexible enough to accommodate new scenarios.  Furthermore, I'm working to integrate the country-specific logic within the XML.

사용자의 요청에 따라 연결 진단 리포트 영역을 개선하여 **CS 답변 자동 생성 기능**을 추가하겠습니다.

**변경 계획:**

1.  **`types.ts`**: CS 진단 유형(`CsDiagnosisType`) 정의를 추가합니다.
2.  **`services/logParser.ts`**:
    *   `analyzeConnection` 함수 내에 4가지 시나리오(HUD 간섭, NO DATA, WiFi 연결, 일반 연결 실패)를 감지하는 로직을 추가합니다.
    *   로그에서 국가 코드(`userCountry`)를 추출하는 로직을 보강합니다.
3.  **`components/Dashboard.tsx`**: `ConnectionStatus` 컴포넌트에 `metadata`를 전달하도록 수정합니다.
4.  **`components/ConnectionStatus.tsx`**:
    *   화면을 2분할(좌측: 기술 진단 / 우측: CS 답변)로 변경합니다.
    *   국가 코드(KR vs 그 외)에 따른 번역 로직을 구현합니다.
    *   제공해주신 답변 템플릿을 적용하고 복사 기능을 추가합니다.

payload.text:
**Examining the Core Logic**

I've been drilling down on refining the interference detection. The initial method, focusing on response length and mixed content post-`ATZ`, proved insufficient. I'm now exploring patterns in the data itself to build a more robust detection mechanism. This includes scrutinizing specific byte sequences and time-based anomalies within the communication flow. The goal is to isolate the true "HUD/Y-Cable" signature.


**Refining Detection Strategies**

I'm now investigating a more precise detection method. The key is to look for CAN frame-like data following the `ATZ` command, specifically, responses starting with common CAN IDs (`7E8`, `7E9`, `7DF`) or containing multiple slashes `/`.  This addresses the previous issue of false positives by focusing on a distinct data pattern.  I'm also streamlining the UI with newline adjustments in the CS response templates for improved readability and have noted that state is naturally reset on app reload.


**Analyzing Reset Behavior**

I'm now focusing on user requests regarding the reset functionality. Initial analysis revealed client-side React apps naturally reset on page refresh, a key insight. After further review, I suspect the user might be seeking a hard reload through the UI's "Reset" button. This could stem from confusion with `vite`'s HMR or a desire to confirm the default reset behavior. My next step will be to differentiate between a soft and hard reset in the design.


**Verifying Reset Mechanics**

I'm now revisiting the "Reset" implementation. After confirming `handleReset` in `App.tsx` clears data appropriately, I'm focusing on user expectations. Examining `Dashboard.tsx`, I see the "Open New File" button calls `onReset`. The user's specific log, `7DF, ATZ : 7E8...`, indicates CAN bus interference. 아, 이게 map이 너무 거대해서 JVM의 최대 사이즈를 넘어가서 빌드가 안 되는 상황을 이미 겪으셨군요.

요청하신 대로 **세 가지 수정 사항**을 반영하겠습니다.

1.  **HUD/Y케이블 간섭 감지 로직 강화**: `ATZ` 명령에 대해 표준 응답(`ELM327 v1.5` 등)이 아닌, `7E8`, `7E9` 등 **CAN 프레임 데이터**가 쏟아지는 패턴을 감지하도록 파서를 수정합니다.
2.  **CS 답변 템플릿 줄바꿈 수정**: 요청하신 대로 서론 아래와 맺음말 위에 공백 라인을 추가하여 가독성을 높입니다.
3.  **새로고침 초기화**: 웹 앱은 기본적으로 새로고침(F5) 시 초기화되지만, 확실하게 하기 위해 로컬 스토리지 등을 사용하지 않음을 확인하고, **새 파일 열기** 버튼 클릭 시에도 모든 내부 캐시를 완벽히 비우도록 합니다.

아래 코드를 업데이트해주세요.

# Updated files:

## services/logParser.ts

-   `analyzeConnection` 함수 내 `hasHudInterference` 로직을 전면 수정했습니다.
-   이제 `ATZ` 명령이 포함된 로그 라인 자체나 그 직후의 로그에서 `7E8`, `7E9`, `7EE`와 같은 **CAN ID 패턴**이 발견되면 즉시 간섭으로 판정합니다. (제공해주신 로그 패턴 `7DF, ATZ : 7E8...` 완벽 대응)

I'm removing the length checks entirely to focus directly on specific CAN identifiers to reduce false positive occurrences.


**Implementing Final Check**

I've simplified and refined the filtering logic by removing broad checks. My new logic targets the core issue, focusing on checking for interference patterns like 7E8, 7E9, or 7EE immediately after an ATZ command. This includes the current log line and the very next line. This approach provides a clearer and more accurate interference detection. I've now completed these improvements, and will move to the next item.


**Simplifying Reset Behavior**

I'm now revising my thinking on the reset action, prompted by user feedback about refresh functionality. My current focus is to ensure a "New File Open" aligns perfectly with a page refresh, addressing potential state persistence. I'm carefully reviewing the codebase to confirm `handleReset` properly clears all cached logs and states. I will clarify reset functionality in `App.tsx` and revisit to confirm reset matches user expectations.


**Implementing Enhanced Interference Checks**

I'm now integrating the updated logic, focusing on "Response values for standard commands" after ATZ, as highlighted. Analyzing the user's "CAN frame as standard" perspective, I'll flag interference if an `ATZ` command is followed by standard CAN IDs, like `7E8`. The immediate `App.tsx` integration will be next.

To address your feedback regarding the HUD interference logic false positive and the formatting requests, I will update the `logParser.ts` and `ConnectionStatus.tsx` files.

**Summary of Changes:**

1.  **`services/logParser.ts`**:
    *   **Fixed HUD/Y-Cable Detection**: Removed the generic check `(msg.includes('/') && msg.length > 40)` which was causing false positives on valid ELM version strings like `ELM327v2.1//>`.
    *   **Strict Pattern Matching**: Now strictly checks for **ATZ** combined with **CAN ID patterns** (`7E8`, `7E9`, `7EE`) either on the same line or the *immediate* next line. This specifically targets the `7DF, ATZ : 7E8...` pattern you provided while ignoring valid ELM responses.
    *   **Reduced Lookahead**: Limited the "next line" check to only the immediate next entry (`index + 1`) to avoid flagging valid responses to subsequent commands (like `0100`) as interference.

2.  **`components/ConnectionStatus.tsx`**:
    *   **Formatting**: Added an extra newline (`\n`) after the opening greeting and before the closing "Thank you" in all CS templates as requested.

3.  **`App.tsx`**:
    *   Verified that the app uses local React state (`useState`) without `localStorage` or URL persistence. This ensures that a **page refresh (F5)** automatically clears all data and returns to the initial file upload screen ("Main Screen") as requested.

entries.description: Refine HUD interference detection to strictly match CAN IDs with ATZ and remove generic length/slash checks
Add spacing to CS response templates for better readability

import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { ConnectionDiagnosis, SessionMetadata, CsDiagnosisType } from '../types'; import { Stethoscope, MessageSquare, Copy, Check, Globe } from 'lucide-react'; interface ConnectionStatusProps { diagnosis: ConnectionDiagnosis; metadata: SessionMetadata; } // CS Response Templates (KR) const CS_RESPONSES_KR: Record = { 'GENERAL_CONNECTION': `안녕하십니까 인포카입니다. 이용에 불편함을 드려 죄송의 말씀 드립니다. \n 고객님께서 프로토콜로 인한 연결 관련 어려움을 겪으신 것으로 보입니다. 네트워크가 원활한 상태에서 설정 -> 스캐너 연결 -> 상단의 "연결 문제 확인하기" 탭을 누르신 뒤 해당 내용 확인 부탁 드립니다. 또한 1. 앱 내 [설정] -> [프로토콜&데이터] 기능을 통해 [응답 대기시간] 및 [재연결 횟수] 늘리기. 2. [프로토콜 종류]에서 프로토콜을 순차적으로 바꾸면서 데이터가 정상적으로 표시되어지는지 확인 부탁 드립니다. (먼저 6번 시도 부탁 드립니다.) 추가적으로 다른 궁금하신 사항이 있으시다면 앱 메인 페이지에서 "사용 가이드" 확인 부탁 드립니다. \n 감사합니다.`, 'NO_DATA_PROTOCOL': `안녕하십니까 인포카입니다. 이용에 불편함을 드려 죄송의 말씀 드립니다. \n 고객님께서 호환되는 프로토콜과 연결이 되지 않아 "NO DATA" 가 응답 되며, 반복적인 비정상 응답으로인한 연결 종료가 된 것으로 보입니다. 아래 사항 참고 부탁 드립니다. 1. 앱 내 [설정] -> [프로토콜&데이터] 기능을 통해 [응답 대기시간] 및 [재연결 횟수] 늘리기. 2. [프로토콜 종류]에서 프로토콜을 순차적으로 바꾸면서 데이터가 정상적으로 표시되어지는지 확인 부탁 드립니다. (먼저 6번 시도 부탁 드립니다.) 그럼에도 데이터가 정상적으로 나오지 않는다면 고객님의 차량과 호환이 되지 않아 데이터가 나오지 않는것으로 판단됩니다. 추가적으로 다른 궁금하신 사항이 있으시다면 앱 메인 페이지에서 "사용 가이드" 확인 부탁 드립니다. \n 감사합니다.`, 'WIFI_CONNECTION': `안녕하세요 인포카입니다. Wi-Fi 모듈 스캐너 연결에 어려움을 겪으신 것으로 보입니다. 이용에 불편함을 드려 죄송의 말씀 드립니다. \n 해당 연결의 경우 1. 디바이스 시스템 설정에서 Wi-Fi 와 Wi-Fi 스캐너와 연결 2. 그 후 인포카 앱 내에서 올바른 IP와 포트 연결 시도 부탁드립니다. 자세한 사항은 앱 내 사용 가이드 - 연결 부분 참고 부탁 드립니다. \n 감사합니다`, 'HUD_INTERFERENCE': `안녕하세요 인포카입니다 먼저 이용에 불편함을 드려서 죄송합니다 \n 보내주신 로그파일을 보았을때에 HUD 제품과 같이사용하시는 것으로 보입니다 HUD 제품과 동시에 사용 시 제품 간 데이터 충돌로 인해 데이터 수집이 어려울 수 있습니다. Y 케이블을 사용하게 되면, 두 가지 제품에서 동시에 데이터 호출하여 ECU에서 정상적인 데이터를 보내지 않을 수 있습니다. obd단자함에 인포카스캐너만 연결해주시기 바랍니다 \n 감사합니다.`, 'SUCCESS': '', 'NONE': '' }; // CS Response Templates (EN - Translated) const CS_RESPONSES_EN: Record = { 'GENERAL_CONNECTION': `Hello, this is Infocar. We apologize for the inconvenience. \n It seems you are experiencing connection difficulties due to protocol issues. Please ensure you have a stable network connection, then go to Settings -> Scanner Connection -> tap the "Check Connection Issues" tab at the top and check the details. Additionally, please try the following: 1. Increase the [Response Timeout] and [Re-connection Count] in the app under [Settings] -> [Protocol & Data]. 2. In [Protocol Type], try changing the protocols sequentially to see if the data is displayed correctly. (Please try number 6 first.) If you have any further questions, please check the "User Guide" on the app's main page. \n Thank you.`, 'NO_DATA_PROTOCOL': `Hello, this is Infocar. We apologize for the inconvenience. \n It appears that "NO DATA" is being returned because a compatible protocol was not established, leading to connection termination due to repeated abnormal responses. Please refer to the following: 1. Increase the [Response Timeout] and [Re-connection Count] in the app under [Settings] -> [Protocol & Data]. 2. In [Protocol Type], try changing the protocols sequentially to see if the data is displayed correctly. (Please try number 6 first.) If data still does not appear, it is determined that the device is not compatible with your vehicle. If you have any further questions, please check the "User Guide" on the app's main page. \n Thank you.`, 'WIFI_CONNECTION': `Hello, this is Infocar. It seems you are having trouble connecting to the Wi-Fi module scanner. We apologize for the inconvenience. \n For this connection, please try the following: 1. Connect to the Wi-Fi scanner in your device's System Settings -> Wi-Fi. 2. Then, attempt to connect using the correct IP and Port within the Infocar app. For more details, please refer to the Connection section in the User Guide within the app. \n Thank you.`, 'HUD_INTERFERENCE': `Hello, this is Infocar. First, we apologize for the inconvenience. \n Looking at the log file you sent, it seems you are using a HUD product together with the scanner. When used simultaneously with a HUD product, data collection may be difficult due to data collision between products. If you use a Y-cable, both products may request data simultaneously, causing the ECU to fail to send normal data. Please connect only the Infocar scanner to the OBD port. \n Thank you.`, 'SUCCESS': '', 'NONE': '' }; export const ConnectionStatus: React.FC = ({ diagnosis, metadata }) => { const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false); // Determine Language based on Country Code const countryCode = metadata.countryCode?.toUpperCase() || 'UNKNOWN'; const isKorea = countryCode === 'KR' || countryCode === 'KOR' || countryCode === '82'; // Select Template const templateMap = isKorea ? CS_RESPONSES_KR : CS_RESPONSES_EN; const csText = templateMap[diagnosis.csType] || ''; const handleCopy = () => { if (!csText) return; navigator.clipboard.writeText(csText); setCopied(true); setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000); }; return (
{/* Left Column: Technical Diagnosis */}

연결 진단 리포트

{diagnosis.summary}

{diagnosis.issues.length > 0 ? (

감지된 이슈 사항

    {diagnosis.issues.map((issue, idx) => (
  • {issue}
    • ))}
) : (
특이 사항이 발견되지 않았습니다.
)}
{/* Right Column: CS Response Generator */}

CS 답변 생성기

{isKorea ? '한국어 (KR)' : `영어 (${countryCode || 'EN'})`}
{csText && ( )}
{csText ? (
{csText}
) : (

생성된 답변이 없습니다.

정상 연결 상태이거나 이슈가 특정되지 않았습니다.

)}
); };

payload.text:
지금 CS 답변 생성기에 컨트리 코드 기준으로 번역한거로 적어달라니까.

예를들어 JP 가 인식 됐으면 일본어로 번역한게 적혀있어야해

**Refining Localization Logic**

I'm currently focused on the `ConnectionStatus.tsx` component and the CS response generation. My update involves incorporating Japanese (JP) localization. The current implementation uses `countryCode` for KR and EN, but I'm now extending it to support JP as well. I'm strategizing how to best integrate the translation logic for Japanese responses within the existing structure.


**Planning Language Expansion**

I'm now expanding my thinking about language support. Based on the user's request for Japanese translation (JP), I've decided to move forward with a dictionary-based translation approach. I'll pre-define translations for common country codes, initially focusing on adding Japanese (JP) to the existing Korean (KR) and English (US, GB, CA, AU) support. I also added Chinese, Russian, and German as potential future targets.


**Optimizing Language Mapping**

I'm now refactoring the `ConnectionStatus` component for cleaner language handling.  I'm switching from separate KR and EN response objects to a language code-keyed map.  A mapping function will translate country codes to language codes, e.g., KR -> KO, JP -> JA. I'm preparing Japanese translations for the four connection scenarios, starting with translating the Korean `GENERAL_CONNECTION` message. The original Korean reads as, "안녕하십니까 인포카입니다..."


**Generating Japanese Responses**

I've just focused on translating the `NO_DATA_PROTOCOL` message into Japanese. The Japanese version now says: "こんにちは、" as a starting point. I'm focusing on crafting a helpful, user-friendly response, building on the previously translated Korean message. I'm aiming for clarity regarding incompatible protocols.


**Composing Japanese Response**

I've crafted a comprehensive Japanese response for a "NO DATA" scenario, including troubleshooting steps. It reads, "こんにちは、インフォカーです。 ご不便をおかけして申し訳ございません。互換性のあるプロトコルと接続できず「NO DATA」が応答され、繰り返しの異常応答により接続が終了したようです。以下をご参照ください。1. アプリ内の [設定] -> [プロトコル＆データ] 機能で [応答待機時間] および [再接続回数] を増やす。2. [プロトコル種類] でプロトコルを順番に変更しながらデータが正常に表示されるか確認する。（まず6番をお試しください。）それでもデータが正常に出ない場合は、お客様の車両と互換性がなくデータが出ないものと判断されます。その他ご不明な点がございましたら、アプリメインページの「使用ガイド」をご確認ください。ありがとうございます。" I'm also starting work on a draft Japanese response for WIFI_CONNECTION.


**Formulating Japanese Reply**

I'm currently crafting a detailed Japanese response for a WIFI_CONNECTION failure. The message addresses connectivity issues, providing clear troubleshooting steps. It advises users to verify Wi-Fi connections in device settings and the Infocar app, referencing the app's guide for further details. I also am looking to address HUD interference issues, and I am drafting a reply for this scenario as well.


**Implementing Japanese Support**

I'm now focusing on the `getLanguageFromCountry` function and how it integrates with `ConnectionStatus.tsx`. I'm going to add logic to map country codes (like 'JP') to the corresponding language code (JA), ensuring a smooth translation lookup. I'm prioritizing the integration of the Japanese translations while maintaining the existing Korean and English templates. I plan to extend the `CsDiagnosisType` if necessary, but will try to keep the design and logic concise. My goal is to ensure that the code is easy to read and doesn't get bloated.


**Adding Country-Based Language Support**

I'm now refining the language selection logic within `getLanguageFromCountry`. Based on the country code provided, I'm integrating support for multiple languages, starting with Japanese (JA) as a key example, and using English (EN) as a default. Currently, I'm strategically expanding the code to accommodate various country codes while prioritizing the user's specific requirement for Japanese localization. I need to be careful and think about how to scale it for additional languages in the future. I'm focusing on ensuring the language selection function can be easily extended and maintained.


**Translating Korean to Japanese**

I am now focusing on translating the Korean `GENERAL_CONNECTION` message into Japanese, aiming for a natural and helpful response. I've begun drafting a Japanese version and will refine it for clarity and user-friendliness. The message will address protocol issues and suggest troubleshooting steps. I am adding this template to the code repository, and making sure to include clear instructions within it.


**Expanding Japanese Translations**

I've just finished translating the `NO_DATA_PROTOCOL` message into Japanese. The result reads: "こんにちは、インフォカーです。 ご利用にご不便をおかけして申し訳ございません。互換性のあるプロトコルと接続できず「NO DATA」が返され、繰り返しの異常応答により接続が終了したようです。以下の事項をご参照ください。1. アプリ内の [設定] -> [プロトコル＆データ] 機能で [応答待機時間] および [再接続回数] を増やしてください。 2. [プロトコル種類] でプロトコルを順番に変更しながらデータが正常に表示されるか確認してください。（まず6番をお試しください。） それでもデータが正常に出ない場合は、お客様の車両と互換性がないためデータが表示されないものと判断". I am moving on to other messages that are relevant.


**Expanding Japanese Messages**

I've just added the completed Japanese responses for `WIFI_CONNECTION` and `HUD_INTERFERENCE` scenarios, including troubleshooting instructions. The `WIFI_CONNECTION` message guides users through device and app Wi-Fi settings. The `HUD_INTERFERENCE` message acknowledges the issue and prompts for log file submission. I'm satisfied with the clarity and user-friendliness of these translations and will now review the updated code.


**Incorporating Japanese Translations**

I'm currently finalizing the integration of the translated Japanese messages within `ConnectionStatus.tsx`. Specifically, I'm addressing potential data conflicts arising from simultaneous use of HUD products, as described by the new user input. I'm focusing on ensuring data integrity when using Y-cables and providing clear instructions to connect only the Infocar scanner to the OBD port. I'm also ensuring the extracted country code is utilized to trigger the Japanese messages.


**Adding Japanese Messages**

I'm now integrating the finalized Japanese message translations, focusing on the country code and font considerations. I'm adding `CS_RESPONSES_JA` to `ConnectionStatus.tsx` and creating a selector function for language-specific messages. Ensuring Japanese character support in the font is my priority to display messages correctly.

사용자의 요청에 따라 `ConnectionStatus.tsx`를 업데이트하여 **국가 코드(Country Code)에 따른 다국어 CS 답변 생성 기능**을 구현하겠습니다.

기존 한국어(KR)와 영어(EN) 외에, 요청하신 **일본어(JP)** 지원을 추가하고, 향후 다른 언어도 쉽게 확장할 수 있도록 구조를 개선합니다.

### 변경 사항
1.  **`components/ConnectionStatus.tsx`**:
    *   **일본어(JP) 템플릿 추가**: `CS_RESPONSES_JA` 객체를 생성하고 4가지 시나리오에 대한 일본어 번역문을 적용했습니다.
    *   **언어 감지 로직 개선**: `metadata.countryCode`를 기반으로 한국어(KR), 일본어(JP), 그 외(EN)를 자동으로 선택하는 함수(`getCsTemplate`)를 구현했습니다.
    *   **UI 업데이트**: 현재 적용된 언어가 무엇인지 표시하는 배지(Badge) 텍스트가 동적으로 변경됩니다.

Y 케이블을 사용하게 되면, 두 가지 제품에서 동시에 데이터 호출하여 ECU에서 정상적인 데이터를 보내지 않을 수 있습니다. obd단자함에 인포카스캐너만 연결해주시기 바랍니다 \n 감사합니다.`, 'SUCCESS': '', 'NONE': '' }; // 2. 영어 (English) const CS_RESPONSES_EN: Record = { 'GENERAL_CONNECTION': `Hello, this is Infocar. We apologize for the inconvenience. \n It seems you are experiencing connection difficulties due to protocol issues. Please ensure you have a stable network connection, then go to Settings -> Scanner Connection -> tap the "Check Connection Issues" tab at the top and check the details. Additionally, please try the following: 1. Increase the [Response Timeout] and [Re-connection Count] in the app under [Settings] -> [Protocol & Data]. 2. In [Protocol Type], try changing the protocols sequentially to see if the data is displayed correctly. (Please try number 6 first.) If you have any further questions, please check the "User Guide" on the app's main page. \n Thank you.`, 'NO_DATA_PROTOCOL': `Hello, this is Infocar. We apologize for the inconvenience. \n It appears that "NO DATA" is being returned because a compatible protocol was not established, leading to connection termination due to repeated abnormal responses. Please refer to the following: 1. Increase the [Response Timeout] and [Re-connection Count] in the app under [Settings] -> [Protocol & Data]. 2. In [Protocol Type], try changing the protocols sequentially to see if the data is displayed correctly. (Please try number 6 first.) If data still does not appear, it is determined that the device is not compatible with your vehicle. If you have any further questions, please check the "User Guide" on the app's main page. \n Thank you.`, 'WIFI_CONNECTION': `Hello, this is Infocar. It seems you are having trouble connecting to the Wi-Fi module scanner. We apologize for the inconvenience. \n For this connection, please try the following: 1. Connect to the Wi-Fi scanner in your device's System Settings -> Wi-Fi. 2. Then, attempt to connect using the correct IP and Port within the Infocar app. For more details, please refer to the Connection section in the User Guide within the app. \n Thank you.`, 'HUD_INTERFERENCE': `Hello, this is Infocar. First, we apologize for the inconvenience. \n Looking at the log file you sent, it seems you are using a HUD product together with the scanner. When used simultaneously with a HUD product, data collection may be difficult due to data collision between products. If you use a Y-cable, both products may request data simultaneously, causing the ECU to fail to send normal data. Please connect only the Infocar scanner to the OBD port. \n Thank you.`, 'SUCCESS': '', 'NONE': '' }; // 3. 일본어 (Japanese) const CS_RESPONSES_JA: Record = { 'GENERAL_CONNECTION': `こんにちは、インフォカーです。 ご利用にご不便をおかけして申し訳ございません。 \n プロトコルの問題により接続が困難になっているようです。 ネットワークが安定した状態で、設定 -> スキャナー接続 -> 上部の「接続問題の確認」タブを押し、該当内容をご確認ください。 また、以下の方法もお試しください。 1. アプリ内の [設定] -> [プロトコル＆データ] 機能で [応答待機時間] および [再接続回数] を増やしてください。 2. [プロトコル種類] でプロトコルを順番に変更しながらデータが正常に表示されるか確認してください。（まず6番をお試しください。） その他ご不明な点がございましたら、アプリメインページの「使用ガイド」をご確認ください。 \n ありがとうございます。`, 'NO_DATA_PROTOCOL': `こんにちは、インフォカーです。 ご利用にご不便をおかけして申し訳ございません。 \n 互換性のあるプロトコルと接続できず「NO DATA」が返され、繰り返しの異常応答により接続が終了したようです。 以下の事項をご参照ください。 1. アプリ内の [設定] -> [プロトコル＆データ] 機能で [応答待機時間] および [再接続回数] を増やしてください。 2. [プロトコル種類] でプロトコルを順番に変更しながらデータが正常に表示されるか確認してください。（まず6番をお試しください。） それでもデータが正常に出ない場合は、お客様の車両と互換性がないためデータが表示されないものと判断されます。 その他ご不明な点がございましたら、アプリメインページの「使用ガイド」をご確認ください。 \n ありがとうございます。`, 'WIFI_CONNECTION': `こんにちは、インフォカーです。 Wi-Fiモジュールスキャナーの接続に問題が発生しているようです。 ご利用にご不便をおかけして申し訳ございません。 \n 当該接続の場合、以下をお試しください。 1. デバイスのシステム設定でWi-FiとWi-Fiスキャナーを接続してください。 2. その後、インフォカーアプリ内で正しいIPとポートで接続を試みてください。 詳細はアプリ内の使用ガイド - 接続部分をご参照ください。 \n ありがとうございます。`, 'HUD_INTERFERENCE': `こんにちは、インフォカーです。 まず、ご利用にご不便をおかけして申し訳ございません。 \n お送りいただいたログファイルを確認したところ、HUD製品と併用されているようです。 HUD製品と同時に使用する場合、製品間のデータ衝突によりデータ収集が困難になることがあります。 Yケーブルを使用すると、2つの製品で同時にデータを呼び出すため、ECUから正常なデータが送信されない場合があります。 OBDポートにはインフォカースキャナーのみを接続してください。 \n ありがとうございます。`, 'SUCCESS': '', 'NONE': '' }; export const ConnectionStatus: React.FC = ({ diagnosis, metadata }) => { const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false); // Determine Language Template based on Country Code const { csText, languageName } = useMemo(() => { const code = metadata.countryCode?.toUpperCase().trim() || 'UNKNOWN'; let templates = CS_RESPONSES_EN; let name = `English (${code})`; // Logic: Select language based on code if (['KR', 'KOR', '82'].includes(code)) { templates = CS_RESPONSES_KR; name = '한국어 (KR)'; } else if (['JP', 'JPN', '81'].includes(code)) { templates = CS_RESPONSES_JA; name = '日本語 (JP)'; } // Add more languages here (e.g., CN, DE, RU) if needed return { csText: templates[diagnosis.csType] || '', languageName: name }; }, [metadata.countryCode, diagnosis.csType]); const handleCopy = () => { if (!csText) return; navigator.clipboard.writeText(csText); setCopied(true); setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000); }; return (
{/* Left Column: Technical Diagnosis */}

연결 진단 리포트

{diagnosis.summary}

{diagnosis.issues.length > 0 ? (

감지된 이슈 사항

    {diagnosis.issues.map((issue, idx) => (
  • {issue}
    • ))}
) : (
특이 사항이 발견되지 않았습니다.
)}
{/* Right Column: CS Response Generator */}

CS 답변 생성기

{languageName}
{csText && ( )}
{csText ? (
{csText}
) : (

생성된 답변이 없습니다.

정상 연결 상태이거나 이슈가 특정되지 않았습니다.

)}
); };

payload.text:
제공하는 언어는 

영어,아랍어,중국어(간체),중국어(번체),프랑스어,독일어,힌디어,인도네시아어,이탈리아어,일본어,한글,말레이어,페르시아어,폴란드어,포르투갈어,러시아어,스페인어,태국어,터키어,우크라이나어,베트남어 이거야. 각각 해당하는 국가 컨트리 코드 별 템플릿 추가하고 번역한거 추가해줘

**Revising Component Logic**

I've been analyzing the request to update `ConnectionStatus.tsx` for the CS Response Generator. My focus is now on implementing language support via country codes. I'm exploring the logic for mapping country codes to the right language formats in the component. I plan to refine the connection display, now that I have a better understanding of the code.


**Adding Language Support**

I'm now diving into the specifics of language mapping within `ConnectionStatus.tsx`.  My priority is creating the function to link country codes, like 'JP' for Japanese or 'KR' for Korean, to the 21 languages specified. I'm focusing on ensuring accurate template translation for each diagnosis type while maintaining the component's existing UI. This includes defining fallbacks and accommodating diverse English variants.


**Establishing Language Mappings**

I've finalized my language mapping strategy for `ConnectionStatus.tsx`.  The plan to define fallbacks and account for variations is now complete. I've focused on using country codes to map to language codes, like `zh-TW` for TW and HK, and `fr` for FR, CA, and BE. Next, I'll generate draft translations following a specific template for consistent messaging.


**Optimizing Template Storage**

I'm revisiting the storage strategy for the language templates. Storing 21 full text blocks for four message types directly within `ConnectionStatus.tsx` risks making the file unwieldy.  To mitigate this, I'm refactoring the code. I'll use a dictionary-like data structure, likely a large configuration object, to store the templates cleanly. This approach will improve readability and maintainability.


**Considering RTL Support**

I'm now thinking about Arabic and Persian. I need to figure out how to handle right-to-left text within the current UI setup using `whitespace-pre-wrap`. I'll stick to standard text content for now and see if basic CSS is enough to display the text correctly. I'll focus on translating the templates into the target languages and will work to make the translations concise and accurate.


**Consolidating Template Data**

I've decided to consolidate the template data into a unified dictionary structure, named `CS_TEMPLATES`, which will replace the separate language constants. I'm focusing now on mapping country codes, like `KR`, `US`, `CN`, `JP`, and others, to their corresponding languages for accurate text selection. The plan is to update the component to select text based on the mapped language and country code. I'm actively drafting translations, considering "Hallo, hier ist Infocar..." for German, "Bonjour, c'est Infocar..." for French, and so on.


**Producing Final Translations**

I'm now generating the large TypeScript file containing all translations, ensuring the requested format and newlines are correct. For Arabic and Persian, I've added `dir="auto"` to the text containers to ensure proper right-to-left text rendering.  My focus is on verifying against user constraints: all language templates are present and translated, satisfying all requirements.  The final XML output is being assembled.

요청하신 21개 언어(영어, 아랍어, 중국어 간체/번체, 프랑스어, 독일어, 힌디어, 인도네시아어, 이탈리아어, 일본어, 한국어, 말레이어, 페르시아어, 폴란드어, 포르투갈어, 러시아어, 스페인어, 태국어, 터키어, 우크라이나어, 베트남어)에 대한 번역 템플릿과 국가 코드 매핑 로직을 `ConnectionStatus.tsx`에 모두 적용했습니다.

또한, 아랍어(AR)나 페르시아어(FA) 같은 RTL(Right-to-Left) 언어가 올바르게 표시되도록 텍스트 컨테이너에 `dir="auto"` 속성을 추가했습니다.

# Updated files:

## components/ConnectionStatus.tsx

-   **`CS_TEMPLATES`**: 모든 언어에 대한 번역 데이터를 포함하는 대형 객체로 구조를 통합했습니다.
-   **`getLanguageFromCountry`**: 국가 코드(예: KR, US, CN, TW, SA 등)를 입력받아 지원하는 언어 키로 변환하는 매핑 로직을 정교하게 구현했습니다.
-   **UI 개선**: 텍스트 영역에 `dir="auto"`를 추가하여 아랍어/페르시아어 등의 가독성을 확보했습니다.

entries.description: Add support for 21 languages with country code mapping and translations

import React, { useState, useMemo } from 'react';
import { ConnectionDiagnosis, SessionMetadata, CsDiagnosisType } from '../types';
import { Stethoscope, MessageSquare, Copy, Check, Globe } from 'lucide-react';

interface ConnectionStatusProps {
  diagnosis: ConnectionDiagnosis;
  metadata: SessionMetadata;
}

type LanguageCode = 
  | 'KO' | 'EN' | 'JA' | 'ZH-CN' | 'ZH-TW' | 'FR' | 'DE' | 'HI' | 'ID' 
  | 'IT' | 'MS' | 'FA' | 'PL' | 'PT' | 'RU' | 'ES' | 'TH' | 'TR' | 'UK' | 'VI' | 'AR';

const LANGUAGE_NAMES: Record<LanguageCode, string> = {
  'KO': '한국어 (Korean)',
  'EN': 'English',
  'JA': '日本語 (Japanese)',
  'ZH-CN': '简体中文 (Chinese Simplified)',
  'ZH-TW': '繁體中文 (Chinese Traditional)',
  'FR': 'Français (French)',
  'DE': 'Deutsch (German)',
  'HI': 'हिन्दी (Hindi)',
  'ID': 'Bahasa Indonesia',
  'IT': 'Italiano (Italian)',
  'MS': 'Bahasa Melayu (Malay)',
  'FA': 'فارسی (Persian)',
  'PL': 'Polski (Polish)',
  'PT': 'Português (Portuguese)',
  'RU': 'Русский (Russian)',
  'ES': 'Español (Spanish)',
  'TH': 'ไทย (Thai)',
  'TR': 'Türkçe (Turkish)',
  'UK': 'Українська (Ukrainian)',
  'VI': 'Tiếng Việt (Vietnamese)',
  'AR': 'العربية (Arabic)',
};

// Unified Template Dictionary
const CS_TEMPLATES: Record<LanguageCode, Record<CsDiagnosisType, string>> = {
  // 1. 한국어 (Korean)
  'KO': {
    'GENERAL_CONNECTION': `안녕하십니까 인포카입니다.
이용에 불편함을 드려 죄송의 말씀 드립니다.
\n
고객님께서 프로토콜로 인한 연결 관련 어려움을 겪으신 것으로 보입니다.
네트워크가 원활한 상태에서 설정 -> 스캐너 연결 -> 상단의 "연결 문제 확인하기" 탭을 누르신 뒤 해당 내용 확인 부탁 드립니다.

또한 
1. 앱 내 [설정] -> [프로토콜&데이터] 기능을 통해 [응답 대기시간] 및 [재연결 횟수] 늘리기.
2. [프로토콜 종류]에서 프로토콜을 순차적으로 바꾸면서 데이터가 정상적으로 표시되어지는지 확인 부탁 드립니다. (먼저 6번 시도 부탁 드립니다.)

추가적으로 다른 궁금하신 사항이 있으시다면 앱 메인 페이지에서 "사용 가이드" 확인 부탁 드립니다.
\n
감사합니다.`,
    'NO_DATA_PROTOCOL': `안녕하십니까 인포카입니다.
이용에 불편함을 드려 죄송의 말씀 드립니다.
\n
고객님께서 호환되는 프로토콜과 연결이 되지 않아 "NO DATA" 가 응답 되며, 반복적인 비정상 응답으로인한 연결 종료가 된 것으로 보입니다.

아래 사항 참고 부탁 드립니다.
1. 앱 내 [설정] -> [프로토콜&데이터] 기능을 통해 [응답 대기시간] 및 [재연결 횟수] 늘리기.
2. [프로토콜 종류]에서 프로토콜을 순차적으로 바꾸면서 데이터가 정상적으로 표시되어지는지 확인 부탁 드립니다. (먼저 6번 시도 부탁 드립니다.)

그럼에도 데이터가 정상적으로 나오지 않는다면 고객님의 차량과 호환이 되지 않아 데이터가 나오지 않는것으로 판단됩니다.

추가적으로 다른 궁금하신 사항이 있으시다면 앱 메인 페이지에서 "사용 가이드" 확인 부탁 드립니다.
\n
감사합니다.`,
    'WIFI_CONNECTION': `안녕하세요 인포카입니다.
Wi-Fi 모듈 스캐너 연결에 어려움을 겪으신 것으로 보입니다.
이용에 불편함을 드려 죄송의 말씀 드립니다.
\n
해당 연결의 경우 
1. 디바이스 시스템 설정에서 Wi-Fi 와 Wi-Fi 스캐너와 연결
2. 그 후 인포카 앱 내에서 올바른 IP와 포트 연결 시도 부탁드립니다.

자세한 사항은 앱 내 사용 가이드 - 연결 부분 참고 부탁 드립니다.
\n
감사합니다`,
    'HUD_INTERFERENCE': `안녕하세요 인포카입니다
먼저 이용에 불편함을 드려서 죄송합니다
\n
보내주신 로그파일을 보았을때에 HUD 제품과 같이사용하시는 것으로 보입니다
HUD 제품과 동시에 사용 시 제품 간 데이터 충돌로 인해 데이터 수집이 어려울 수 있습니다.
Y 케이블을 사용하게 되면, 두 가지 제품에서 동시에 데이터 호출하여 ECU에서 정상적인 데이터를 보내지 않을 수 있습니다.
obd단자함에 인포카스캐너만 연결해주시기 바랍니다
\n
감사합니다.`,
    'SUCCESS': '',
    'NONE': ''
  },

  // 2. English (Default)
  'EN': {
    'GENERAL_CONNECTION': `Hello, this is Infocar.
We apologize for the inconvenience.
\n
It seems you are experiencing connection difficulties due to protocol issues.
Please ensure you have a stable network connection, then go to Settings -> Scanner Connection -> tap the "Check Connection Issues" tab at the top and check the details.

Additionally, please try the following:
1. Increase the [Response Timeout] and [Re-connection Count] in the app under [Settings] -> [Protocol & Data].
2. In [Protocol Type], try changing the protocols sequentially to see if the data is displayed correctly. (Please try number 6 first.)

If you have any further questions, please check the "User Guide" on the app's main page.
\n
Thank you.`,
    'NO_DATA_PROTOCOL': `Hello, this is Infocar.
We apologize for the inconvenience.
\n
It appears that "NO DATA" is being returned because a compatible protocol was not established, leading to connection termination due to repeated abnormal responses.

Please refer to the following:
1. Increase the [Response Timeout] and [Re-connection Count] in the app under [Settings] -> [Protocol & Data].
2. In [Protocol Type], try changing the protocols sequentially to see if the data is displayed correctly. (Please try number 6 first.)

If data still does not appear, it is determined that the device is not compatible with your vehicle.

If you have any further questions, please check the "User Guide" on the app's main page.
\n
Thank you.`,
    'WIFI_CONNECTION': `Hello, this is Infocar.
It seems you are having trouble connecting to the Wi-Fi module scanner.
We apologize for the inconvenience.
\n
For this connection, please try the following:
1. Connect to the Wi-Fi scanner in your device's System Settings -> Wi-Fi.
2. Then, attempt to connect using the correct IP and Port within the Infocar app.

For more details, please refer to the Connection section in the User Guide within the app.
\n
Thank you.`,
    'HUD_INTERFERENCE': `Hello, this is Infocar.
First, we apologize for the inconvenience.
\n
Looking at the log file you sent, it seems you are using a HUD product together with the scanner.
When used simultaneously with a HUD product, data collection may be difficult due to data collision between products.
If you use a Y-cable, both products may request data simultaneously, causing the ECU to fail to send normal data.
Please connect only the Infocar scanner to the OBD port.
\n
Thank you.`,
    'SUCCESS': '',
    'NONE': ''
  },

  // 3. Japanese
  'JA': {
    'GENERAL_CONNECTION': `こんにちは、インフォカーです。
ご利用にご不便をおかけして申し訳ございません。
\n

  // 6. French
  'FR': {
    'GENERAL_CONNECTION': `Bonjour, c'est Infocar.

  // 7. German
  'DE': {
    'GENERAL_CONNECTION': `Hallo, hier ist Infocar.
Wir entschuldigen uns für die Unannehmlichkeiten.
\n
Es scheint, dass Sie aufgrund von Protokollproblemen Verbindungsschwierigkeiten haben.
Bitte stellen Sie sicher, dass Sie eine stabile Netzwerkverbindung haben, gehen Sie dann zu Einstellungen -> Scannerverbindung -> tippen Sie oben auf den Tab "Verbindungsprobleme prüfen" und überprüfen Sie die Details.

Bitte versuchen Sie zusätzlich Folgendes:
1. Erhöhen Sie das [Antwort-Timeout] und die [Anzahl der Wiederverbindungen] in der App unter [Einstellungen] -> [Protokoll & Daten].
2. Ändern Sie unter [Protokolltyp] die Protokolle nacheinander, um zu sehen, ob die Daten korrekt angezeigt werden. (Bitte versuchen Sie zuerst Nummer 6.)

Wenn Sie weitere Fragen haben, lesen Sie bitte das "Benutzerhandbuch" auf der Hauptseite der App.
\n
Danke.`,

1. Erhöhen Sie das [Antwort-Timeout] und die [Anzahl der Wiederverbindungen] in der App unter [Einstellungen] -> [Protokoll & Daten].

Wenn immer noch keine Daten angezeigt werden, ist das Gerät nicht mit Ihrem Fahrzeug kompatibel.

Wenn Sie weitere Fragen haben, lesen Sie bitte das "Benutzerhandbuch" auf der Hauptseite der App.

بالإضافة إلى ذلك، يرجى تجربة ما يلي:

إذا كان لديك أي أسئلة أخرى، يرجى التحقق من "دليل المستخدم" في الصفحة الرئيسية للتطبيق.

شكرًا لك.`,
    'NO_DATA_PROTOCOL': `مرحبًا، هذه Infocar.
نعتذر عن الإزعاج.

يبدو أنه يتم إرجاع "NO DATA" لعدم إنشاء بروتوكول متوافق، مما يؤدي إلى إنهاء الاتصال بسبب الاستجابات غير الطبيعية المتكررة.

const getLanguageFromCountry = (countryCode: string | undefined): LanguageCode => {
  if (!countryCode) return 'EN';
  
  const code = countryCode.toUpperCase().trim();
  
  // Direct Matches
  if (['KR', 'KOR', '82'].includes(code)) return 'KO';
  if (['JP', 'JPN', '81'].includes(code)) return 'JA';
  if (['CN', 'CHN', '86'].includes(code)) return 'ZH-CN';
  if (['TW', 'TWN', 'HK', 'HKG', '886', '852'].includes(code)) return 'ZH-TW';
  if (['FR', 'FRA', 'BE', 'BEL', '33'].includes(code)) return 'FR';
  if (['DE', 'DEU', 'AT', 'AUT', 'CH', 'CHE', '49'].includes(code)) return 'DE';
  if (['IN', 'IND', '91'].includes(code)) return 'HI';
  if (['ID', 'IDN', '62'].includes(code)) return 'ID';
  if (['IT', 'ITA', '39'].includes(code)) return 'IT';
  if (['MY', 'MYS', '60'].includes(code)) return 'MS';
  if (['IR', 'IRN', '98'].includes(code)) return 'FA';
  if (['PL', 'POL', '48'].includes(code)) return 'PL';
  if (['PT', 'PRT', 'BR', 'BRA', '351', '55'].includes(code)) return 'PT';
  if (['RU', 'RUS', 'KZ', 'KAZ', '7'].includes(code)) return 'RU';
  if (['ES', 'ESP', 'MX', 'MEX', 'AR', 'ARG', 'CO', 'COL', '34'].includes(code)) return 'ES';
  if (['TH', 'THA', '66'].includes(code)) return 'TH';
  if (['TR', 'TUR', '90'].includes(code)) return 'TR';
  if (['UA', 'UKR', '380'].includes(code)) return 'UK';
  if (['VN', 'VNM', '84'].includes(code)) return 'VI';
  if (['SA', 'SAU', 'AE', 'ARE', 'EG', 'EGY', 'IQ', 'IRQ', 'QA', 'QAT', 'JO', 'JOR', 'KW', 'KWT', 'OM', 'OMN'].includes(code)) return 'AR';
  
  // Default to English for US, GB, CA, AU, etc. and unknown
  return 'EN';
};

export const ConnectionStatus: React.FC<ConnectionStatusProps> = ({ diagnosis, metadata }) => {
  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);

  // Determine Language Template based on Country Code
  const { csText, languageName, langCode } = useMemo(() => {
    const code = getLanguageFromCountry(metadata.countryCode);
    
    return {
      csText: CS_TEMPLATES[code][diagnosis.csType] || '',
      languageName: LANGUAGE_NAMES[code],
      langCode: code
    };
  }, [metadata.countryCode, diagnosis.csType]);

  const handleCopy = () => {
    if (!csText) return;
    navigator.clipboard.writeText(csText);
    setCopied(true);
    setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000);
  };

  const isRTL = langCode === 'AR' || langCode === 'FA';

  return (
    <div className="bg-white rounded-lg border border-slate-200 mb-6 shadow-sm overflow-hidden">
      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 divide-y md:divide-y-0 md:divide-x divide-slate-200">
        
        {/* Left Column: Technical Diagnosis */}
        <div className="p-6">
            <div className="flex items-start gap-4 h-full">
                <div className="mt-1">
                    <Stethoscope className="w-8 h-8 text-slate-400" />
                </div>
                <div className="flex-1">
                <h3 className="text-lg font-bold text-slate-900 mb-1">
                    연결 진단 리포트
                </h3>
                <p className="text-slate-700 font-medium mb-3">{diagnosis.summary}</p>

                {diagnosis.issues.length > 0 ? (
                    <div className="bg-slate-50 rounded-md p-4 border border-slate-200">
                    <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-slate-500 mb-3 uppercase">감지된 이슈 사항</p>
                    <ul className="space-y-2">
                        {diagnosis.issues.map((issue, idx) => (
                        <li key={idx} className="flex items-start text-sm text-slate-700 gap-2">
                            <span className="mt-2 w-1.5 h-1.5 bg-slate-400 rounded-full shrink-0" />
                            {issue}
                        </li>
                        ))}
                    </ul>
                    </div>
                ) : (
                    <div className="text-sm text-slate-500">
                        특이 사항이 발견되지 않았습니다.
                    </div>
                )}
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>

        {/* Right Column: CS Response Generator */}
        <div className="p-6 bg-slate-50/50">
            <div className="flex flex-col h-full">
                <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-4">
                     <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
                        <MessageSquare className="w-5 h-5 text-indigo-600" />
                        <h3 className="text-base font-bold text-slate-900">CS 답변 생성기</h3>
                     </div>
                     <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
                        <div className="flex items-center gap-1 text-[10px] bg-white border border-slate-200 px-2 py-1 rounded-full text-slate-500">
                             <Globe className="w-3 h-3" />
                             <span>{languageName}</span>
                        </div>
                        {csText && (
                            <button 
                                onClick={handleCopy}
                                className={`flex items-center gap-1.5 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-md text-xs font-bold transition-all shadow-sm border ${
                                    copied 
                                    ? 'bg-green-100 text-green-700 border-green-200' 
                                    : 'bg-white text-slate-700 border-slate-300 hover:bg-slate-50 hover:text-indigo-600'
                                }`}
                            >
                                {copied ? <Check className="w-3 h-3" /> : <Copy className="w-3 h-3" />}
                                {copied ? '복사됨' : '복사'}
                            </button>
                        )}
                     </div>
                </div>

                <div className="flex-1">
                    {csText ? (
                         <div 
                            dir="auto"
                            className={`w-full h-full min-h-[160px] bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-lg p-3 text-xs text-slate-700 font-mono whitespace-pre-wrap overflow-y-auto max-h-[300px] ${isRTL ? 'text-right' : 'text-left'}`}
                         >
                            {csText}
                         </div>
                    ) : (
                        <div className="w-full h-full min-h-[160px] flex flex-col items-center justify-center text-slate-400 border border-dashed border-slate-300 rounded-lg bg-slate-50">
                            <p className="text-sm">생성된 답변이 없습니다.</p>
                            <p className="text-xs mt-1">정상 연결 상태이거나 이슈가 특정되지 않았습니다.</p>
                        </div>
                    )}
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>

      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

The `scanAttempted` condition is being meticulously reviewed. I'm focusing on the assumption that discovering "WI FI OBD" inherently implies a scan.
